wada_substances.name: Testosterone
wada_substances.notes: Endogenous anabolic steroid; triggers positive if ratio >4:1.

wada_substances.name: Nandrolone
wada_substances.notes: Found in contaminated meat and some herbal supplements.

wada_substances.name: Stanozolol
wada_substances.notes: Common in counterfeit mass-gainer powders sold in rural markets.

wada_substances.name: Clostebol
wada_substances.notes: Present in some topical creams/ointments used for wound healing.

wada_substances.name: Clenbuterol
wada_substances.notes: Found in contaminated meat; also misused as a fat-burner.

wada_substances.name: Salbutamol (Albuterol)
wada_substances.notes: Permitted by inhalation up to 1600 mcg/day for asthma. Needs TUE for oral.

wada_substances.name: EPO (Erythropoietin)
wada_substances.notes: Blood-boosting agent; banned in all sports at all times.

wada_substances.name: HGH (Human Growth Hormone)
wada_substances.notes: Banned in-competition and out-of-competition.

wada_substances.name: Methylhexaneamine (DMAA)
wada_substances.notes: Derived from geranium oil; found in pre-workouts and cosmetics.

wada_substances.name: Ephedrine
wada_substances.notes: Present in traditional Chinese medicine and some cold remedies.

wada_substances.name: Pseudoephedrine
wada_substances.notes: Common decongestant in over-the-counter cold medicines.

wada_substances.name: Amphetamine
wada_substances.notes: Some ADHD medications contain amphetamine – TUE needed.

wada_substances.name: Furosemide
wada_substances.notes: Used medically for edema; banned in sport as a masking agent.

wada_substances.name: Probenecid
wada_substances.notes: Gout medicine that can mask steroid use – always banned.

wada_substances.name: Prednisolone
wada_substances.notes: Common anti-inflammatory; banned in-competition without TUE.

wada_substances.name: Dexamethasone
wada_substances.notes: Frequently prescribed by rural doctors; triggers positive test.

wada_substances.name: Betamethasone
wada_substances.notes: Topical and injectable steroid; banned in-competition.

wada_substances.name: Cannabinoids (THC)
wada_substances.notes: Banned in-competition only. CBD is NOT prohibited.

wada_substances.name: Insulin
wada_substances.notes: Banned for non-diabetic athletes. Diabetic athletes need TUE.

wada_substances.name: Ibuprofen
wada_substances.notes: NOT banned. Safe for athletes to use as pain relief.

wada_substances.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
wada_substances.notes: NOT banned. Safe to use for fever and pain.

wada_substances.name: Creatine Monohydrate
wada_substances.notes: NOT banned by WADA. Widely used and generally safe if certified.

wada_substances.name: Caffeine
wada_substances.notes: NOT banned. Monitored substance – high doses flag for investigation.

medicines.notes: Contains Pseudoephedrine – BANNED stimulant. Avoid completely.
Contains glucocorticoid and beta-2 agonist – TUE required.
Safe – no banned substance. But check variant; some versions have codeine.
Glucocorticoid injection – banned in-competition without TUE.

supplements.name: Ashwagandha
supplements.notes: Contains plant steroids (withanolides) that can trigger anabolic agent positives. Contamination risk very high.

supplements.name: Tribulus Terrestris
supplements.notes: Contains steroidal saponins; linked to testosterone positives. AVOID.

supplements.name: Deer Antler Velvet
supplements.notes: Contains IGF-1 (insulin-like growth factor) – banned peptide hormone. NEVER use.

supplements.name: Shilajit
supplements.notes: Heavy metals found in many samples. May contain undeclared steroids.

supplements.name: Kaunch Beej (Mucuna Pruriens)
supplements.notes: Contains L-DOPA; may affect dopamine and trigger positive for controlled substances.

supplements.name: Whey Protein (local unbranded)
supplements.notes: Unbranded local whey frequently adulterated with anabolic steroids and cheap fillers.

supplements.name: Mass Gainer (counterfeit)
supplements.notes: Counterfeit mass gainers often contain banned anabolic agents and diuretics.

supplements.name: Optimum Nutrition Gold Standard Whey
supplements.notes: Internationally certified brand. Relatively safe but buy from verified retailers only.

supplements.name: MuscleBlaze Whey Protein (CoE-NSTS certified)
supplements.notes: CoE-NSTS batch tested and certified. Safe for Indian athletes.

supplements.name: Creatine Monohydrate (Bigmuscles / CoE-NSTS)
supplements.notes: Creatine itself is not banned. CoE-NSTS certified batch – safe.

supplements.name: Geranium Oil / Geranamine
supplements.notes: Natural source of DMAA (Methylhexaneamine) – BANNED stimulant. Found in cosmetics too.

supplements.name: Pre-workout (local unbranded)
supplements.notes: Unverified pre-workouts frequently contain DMAA, ephedrine, or banned stimulants.

supplements.name: Sarpagandha (Rauwolfia)
supplements.notes: Contains reserpine and yohimbine – potential stimulant flagging.

supplements.name: Multivitamin (Revital H / CoE-NSTS)
supplements.notes: Standard multivitamin – if CoE-NSTS certified batch, safe to use.

supplements.name: Iron Supplements (Ferrous Sulphate)
supplements.notes: NOT banned. Safe for anemic athletes. Prefer pharmaceutical grade.

supplements.name: Vitamin D3 (Calcirol)
supplements.notes: NOT banned. Safe and commonly needed in India.
